project: Royal Caribbean DAM — Bulk Enrichment & Expanded Filters
overview: Enrich all 49 existing DAM assets with AI-generated metadata via a re-runnable CLI script, then expand the filter sidebar to expose enriched dimensions (channel, scene) so content authors can find assets by AI-inferred context.
goals: Bulk-enrich all unenriched assets using OpenAI GPT-4o Vision via a CLI script
Retry failed enrichments once with a simplified prompt before skipping
Expand GET /api/filters and GET /api/assets to support channel and scene filter params
Add Channel and Scene/Mood filter sections to the React filter sidebar
nonGoals: No changes to single-asset upload enrichment flow
No UI trigger for bulk enrichment — CLI only
No editing or updating of .content.xml source files
No authentication or rate-limit UI feedback
successMetrics: Running `node server/scripts/enrich-all.js` enriches all 49 assets and prints a summary
GET /api/filters returns non-empty `channels` and `scenes` arrays after enrichment
GET /api/assets?channel=hero returns only assets with enriched_channel='hero'
Channel and Scene filter sections appear in the UI after enrichment populates data

routes.name: Filter Options
routes.purpose: Now returns channels and scenes arrays in addition to existing fields

routes.name: Search Assets
routes.purpose: Now accepts channel and scene query params

uiNotes: Channel filter: chip group (hero, banner, mobile, desktop, square) — only render section if channels array is non-empty
Scene/Mood filter: chip group of distinct enriched_scene values — only render section if scenes array is non-empty
Both new sections follow the same collapsible pattern as existing FilterSidebar sections
Active channel/scene filters shown as removable chips below search bar (same pattern as existing active filters)

dataModel.description: No schema changes — enriched_channel and enriched_scene columns already exist
fields: enriched_channel TEXT — set by enrich-all script
enriched_scene TEXT — set by enrich-all script
enrichment_source TEXT — set to 'ai-vision' after successful enrichment

rules: Script targets assets WHERE enrichment_source IS NULL — re-running skips already-enriched assets
Image source priority: (1) fetch Scene7 CDN URL = scene7_domain.replace(/\/+$/, '') + '/is/image/' + scene7_file; (2) fall back to path.resolve(DATA_DIR, thumbnail_path) if file exists on disk
If no image source is available (no scene7_file and no thumbnail_path), skip asset and log 'no image source'
Full prompt asks for: title, description, tags (array), location, scene, subjects (array), mood, channel_hint, confidence
Retry prompt (simplified): asks only for title, description, tags (array), location, scene, channel_hint, confidence — omits mood and subjects
After retry failure, log 'FAILED: <filename>' and continue — do not exit
ENRICH_DELAY_MS env var (default 500) — wait this many ms between each asset call
FTS5 sync: after each successful DB update, run INSERT INTO assets_fts(...) to keep search in sync
GET /api/filters channels: SELECT DISTINCT enriched_channel FROM assets WHERE enriched_channel IS NOT NULL ORDER BY enriched_channel
GET /api/filters scenes: SELECT DISTINCT enriched_scene FROM assets WHERE enriched_scene IS NOT NULL ORDER BY enriched_scene

stories.title: Bulk enrichment CLI script
stories.description: As a developer, I want a re-runnable CLI script that enriches all unenriched assets with OpenAI Vision so the full 49-asset library has AI-generated metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: File created at server/scripts/enrich-all.js — executable via `node server/scripts/enrich-all.js` from the app/ root
Script loads .env from app root using dotenv (require('dotenv').config({ path: path.resolve(__dirname, '../../.env') }))
Script opens dam.sqlite using better-sqlite3 at path.resolve(__dirname, '../../dam.sqlite')
Queries SELECT * FROM assets WHERE enrichment_source IS NULL to get unenriched assets

For each asset: (1) tries to fetch image from Scene7 CDN URL as base64; (2) if fetch fails or no scene7_file, falls back to reading thumbnail file from DATA_DIR (default ../Data/royal relative to server/)
If neither image source is available, logs 'SKIP <filename>: no image source' and continues
Sends image to OpenAI GPT-4o Vision with full prompt (title, description, tags, location, scene, subjects, mood, channel_hint, confidence)
On OpenAI error: retries once with simplified prompt (title, description, tags, location, scene, channel_hint, confidence only)
On second failure: logs 'FAILED <filename>: <error message>' and continues to next asset

On success: updates assets row with enriched_title, enriched_description, enriched_tags (JSON), enriched_location, enriched_scene, enriched_subjects (JSON), enriched_mood, enriched_channel, enrichment_source='ai-vision', enrichment_confidence
After each successful update, syncs FTS5: DELETE FROM assets_fts WHERE id=? then INSERT INTO assets_fts(id,title,description,tags,location,creator,category,subcategory,filename) VALUES(...)
Waits ENRICH_DELAY_MS (default 500) between each asset
Logs progress: '[X/49] Enriching <filename>... done' or '... retrying...' or '... failed' or '... skipped'
Prints summary on completion: 'Done. Enriched: X | Failed: Y | Skipped: Z'
Example: running script with valid OPENAI_API_KEY processes all 49 assets and prints summary
Negative case: if OPENAI_API_KEY is missing, script logs 'Error: OPENAI_API_KEY not set' and exits with code 1 immediately
Negative case: re-running script after all assets are enriched logs 'All assets already enriched. Nothing to do.' and exits 0

stories.title: Expand /api/filters and /api/assets for channel and scene
stories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the filters and assets API endpoints to support channel and scene so the UI can filter by AI-enriched dimensions.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/filters response includes `channels`: array of distinct non-null enriched_channel values sorted alphabetically
GET /api/filters response includes `scenes`: array of distinct non-null enriched_scene values sorted alphabetically
GET /api/assets accepts `channel` query param — adds WHERE enriched_channel = :channel to query
GET /api/assets accepts `scene` query param — adds WHERE enriched_scene = :scene to query
Both new filters compose correctly with existing filters (category, rights_status, q, etc.)
Supertest test: GET /api/filters returns channels and scenes as arrays (may be empty before enrichment)
Supertest test: after inserting a test asset with enriched_channel='hero', GET /api/filters includes 'hero' in channels
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?channel=hero returns only assets with enriched_channel='hero'
Supertest test: GET /api/assets?scene=ocean returns only assets with enriched_scene='ocean'
Negative case: GET /api/assets?channel=nonexistent returns { total: 0, assets: [] } with 200 status

stories.title: Add Channel and Scene filter sections to FilterSidebar
stories.description: As a content author, I want to filter assets by AI-inferred channel and scene so I can quickly find the right format for my campaign.
acceptanceCriteria: FilterSidebar renders a 'Channel' collapsible section below existing sections — only rendered if filters.channels is non-empty
Channel chips: one chip per value in filters.channels — clicking selects/deselects (single-select, same as category)
FilterSidebar renders a 'Scene / Mood' collapsible section — only rendered if filters.scenes is non-empty
Scene chips: multi-select chips from filters.scenes — clicking toggles inclusion
Both sections start collapsed by default
Active channel selection shown as a removable chip in the active filters row below the search bar
Active scene selections shown as removable chips in the active filters row
Clearing a chip deselects the filter and re-fetches assets
getAssets() call in BrowsePage includes channel and scene params when set
Vitest test: FilterSidebar with channels=['hero','banner'] renders a 'Channel' section with two chips
Vitest test: FilterSidebar with channels=[] does not render a 'Channel' section
Vitest test: clicking a channel chip calls onFilterChange with key='channel' and the chip value
Negative case: if API returns no channels and no scenes, FilterSidebar renders identically to before enrichment (no empty sections)

stories.title: Fix dotenv path and update enrich-all script to use Anthropic
stories.description: As a developer, I want the server to correctly load .env and the enrich-all script to use the Anthropic Claude API so enrichment works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: In server/scripts/enrich-all.js, replace any OpenAI references with Anthropic: require('@anthropic-ai/sdk') instead of openai, use anthropicClient.messages.create() with model 'claude-opus-4-6'
Message format: content array with { type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', media_type: mimeType, data: base64Data } } and { type: 'text', text: prompt }
Extract response text from response.content[0].text (not response.choices[0].message.content)
Script reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env (not OPENAI_API_KEY) — exits with error if missing
Simplified retry prompt also uses Anthropic client with same message format
Example: running script with valid ANTHROPIC_API_KEY successfully enriches assets
Negative case: running without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY logs 'Error: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY not set' and exits 1
